place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Nice and quiet.  Library staff are helpful. But mobile data signal by Simba ( 1 bar inside, 2 -3 bars outside. not sure about the others) is really poor. Couldn't even login to "checking and secure my seat booking", thus the booking got canceled,  and went to someone else.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Queenstown library is a heritage building, rich in its own history. Spacious with two levels, kids area is on the first floor. The second floor for adults is really quiet with nice view over a green park.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
building's exterior may look unassuming or even a lil spooky but the inside is really cozy and homely! lovely space, limited tables but plenty of seats

if you really want a table, they have a table booking system you can use to ensure you have one when you get there!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Nostalgia a plenty in this well preserved library that houses many childhood memories for some.

Located in the quiet side of Queenstown facing sky residences @ Dawson, short walk from Queenstown MRT, a cozy library to spend your time here.

2 levels served by a lift & stairs, lots of reading areas and I particularly enjoy the level 2 quiet reading room facing the greenery.

There are lots of nostalgia and nice touches to this library.

Take your time to explore, read, borrow or exchange. what a privilege!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
As of April 2026: It is a old school library with the kinda of nostalgia you get when you visited a library in the 2000's.

Most of the Library's floor space is dedicated to books with working space being rather limited. It's not very convenient to access either as you gotta walk abit from the MRT station and not much amenities nearby.

The tables tend to get booked out until like 7pm ish. With patrons playing musical chairs where someone who has a booking asks those who didn't make a booking to move.

Interesting mix of patrons, with elderly men being the majority and somehow quite a few expats.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Came here for a toastmasters meeting. Great facilities and ambiance. It looks like an engaging and well operated community Center.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Thank you Veronica for helping me to settle the Singpass matters. Also Karen too for your help re the SG seniors vouchers. These are 2 beautiful, kind n patient souls!

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
Has a sheltered basketball court that is used for everything except basketball. Ring is missing on one side of the court.

Had so much potential as one of the only few sheltered basketball courts in the area but unfortunately poorly maintained and not used to its potential.

Can you imagine a basketball court that is booked every evening (except Tuesdays) for non-basketball related activities? This is it.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Appealing for CDC voucher, take almost 1 month didn't approve.  Call every single day,the staffs kept give the reason * in- charge is not in the office * . Just wondering when the in charge will be in the office,? And when the voucher will be issued???

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: I went over to do some collection and update certain issues. I was served by Mr Stanley Tan who was outstanding in his customer handling. He was patient and always answered my questions well and it showed his good product knowledge. He was courteous and a credit to your CC. Kindly convey my thanks to him

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: I alighted at this bus interchange (the newer one as opposed to the older "Temporary" one) getting off Bus number 961 from Hillview where I live. I arrived here to visit Causeway Point that is just "above and next to" this bus interchange. Unlike the older "Temporary" bus interchange, this one is new and nicer in terms of comfort as it is air-conditioned and access to Causeway Point is by a convenient set of escalators.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: The bus driver seemed half asleep; he was driving incredibly slowly. A bus is meant to get people to their destination quickly, not to make them late! This is terrible! Does he not know how to drive a bus, or is he just sleepy?

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
so much space, which is good. my only complaint is the main passenger alighting area .. where it can be quite a distance to walk all the way towards the direction of the MRT Station.

there's also this food stall simply named "Nasi Padang" (01-24) which serves surprisingly good tasting malay food at decent prices.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Decent spot for variety, but not for those craving familiar Pakistani flavours
Visited Lau Pa Sat (Lay Pa Sat) recently in Singapore. It’s a lively and iconic hawker centre with plenty of options and a nice atmosphere, especially in the evening when the streets are closed off for dining.
We tried the halal stall and ordered chicken rice noodles, chicken basil vegetables, and tea tree something. While the portions were generous and the service was quick, the overall taste was quite different from what we expected. Everything had a strong seafood-like flavour profile (even the non-seafood items), which didn’t really appeal to us as I can’t stand sea food smell. It felt quite far from Pakistani taste preferences – the spices, seasoning, and balance were very different.
If you’re in the mood to explore local Singaporean or Southeast Asian hawker food and try new things, Lau Pa Sat is a great place with lots of variety. However, if you’re particular about your food and want something closer to Pakistani cuisine, this might not be the best choice.
Cleanliness was just fine for a busy hawker centre, and the pricing was reasonable. Good for tourists or casual explorers, but we probably won’t rush back.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: fantastic place to grab some food and/or beer. Famous for the skewers on the street, especially the chicken satay. Plenty of options to choose from, Indian, Arabic, Chinese, Singaporean, etc. There's something for everyone. Good ventilation and plenty of tables but the place is always packed and noisy (it doesn't take away the enjoyment). A must to visit.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: There's no need to write much about this place; you simply must stop in for a fantastic meal. There are plenty of street food joints offering a variety of Asian cuisines. Everything is perfectly seasoned, with plenty to choose from, so everyone should easily find something to their liking. And the prices are very affordable by Singaporean standards.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Lau Pa Sat: A must-visit for the lively night vibe!

I visited at 9 PM on a Friday, and it was absolutely crowded! The energy outside on Satay Street was incredible, with everyone enjoying grilled food in the open air. Inside the historic building, there are plenty of food stalls to choose from.

The Drinks:
One thing to watch out for is that there are very few drink stalls, so the lines are very long. I bought an Iced Mango Peach ($2.80), but I wouldn't recommend it. It was extremely sweet and didn't contain any fresh fruit—it tasted mostly like syrup. I suggest skipping the "juices" here if you want something fresh.

The Food & Service:
I tried the Thunder Tea Rice ($6.20). It was okay, though it didn't quite fit my personal taste. After eating, the service is efficient; you can either clear your own tray or wait for the staff who come by frequently to collect them.

The Verdict:
It is very crowded and noisy with loud music, but it’s a "must-visit" to experience the local Singaporean vibe. Just be prepared for the crowds!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Tried Na Na Curry at Lau Pa Sat because saw that they are awarded Michelin Bib Gourmand 10 years in a row!

Their chicken curry is rich, slightly creamy, and super comforting. The spices are well-balanced, not too heavy but still very satisfying.

The chicken is tender and soaks up the curry nicely, making every bite flavourful. Pairs really well with both the rice and roti prata!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
7 Feb 2026 2pm Unexpected detour that we met this halal gem. Delicious food n good vibes. Chicken salad is tender n seasoned well. Mash potato on point. Pasta with right amount of sauce n flavours....yummy....quench with lemongrass tea....refreshingly awesome! Staff is friendly n fast service. Friendly lady boss too. Oh btw we came via walk in n thank god there is a table for us yeay! Almost full house!
Just hope they change their system to order n pay at the same time upon order via QR code as business in 2026 hard to stay a float in Singapore. Would be sad to have this kind of local halal gem lost.
Recommended!
Sorry to missed out the good portion of chicken salad...my MIL already dive right in after the sight of the mouth watering chicken hehe....
They are at the entrance at Active SG Gym

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: I was very happy with my experience here. I was shocked to see so much reviews for this restaurant,but when the food arrived, I understood why the review was so high. The food was in a reasonable price, came in a very generous amount, and tasted great. I ordered the carbonara, tom yam pasta (extra spicy for the kick), grilled chicken and the burger. For dessert, I got the choco-lava cake and cookie milkshake. For people who are craving something sweet, I recommend trying the cookie milkshake. The place felt cozy and inviting. I truly recommend trying the food here!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
The generous portions here really stood out to me. Even the House Salad came with a really huge grilled chicken thigh. Tried the Thai Green Curry Seafood Pasta which was packed with ingredients along with their Fried Chicken & Waffles and Thai crispy fish with Thai chilli sauce too.

The giant milkshakes were one of the highlights and the molten lava cake made a nice ending to the meal. Overall a nice casual spot to gather with friends plus it’s halal certified and opens till 5am daily too.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Decided to treat my friend for her birthday at a place which i believe both of us could enjoy. Heard of this place since 2022 i believe but never had a chance to come previously. When i step in, i could feel good vibes with a good choice of song list i could say, clean and cozy with nice decor. We've been greeted and attended by Azrul upon our arrival and  been ushered to out seat. While seating i found the atmosphere was very nice and calm/chill which very comforting. I find their menu is very interesting since they offered Thai and Western food (some even a fusion of both). After ordering our food, i approached their counter to ask about the cakes that they offered and been attended by Sheryln, since i wanted to surprise my friend and Sheryln guide the how to. Overall we were stuffed with good foods served by both Azrul and Sheryln which is quite friendly and professional. Its definitely a good choice on my side (self praised, lol).

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
First time here. Called to check if reservation is needed, straightaway reserved seats for my family. Wonderful!

The mains here are all flavourful & the portions are HUGE for 2 persons... See the pics for yourself. Come here for the chill vibes, definitely NO REGRETS!!!

review_text:
The generous portions here really stood out to me. Even the House Salad came with a really huge grilled chicken thigh. Tried the Thai Green Curry Seafood Pasta which was packed with ingredients along with their Fried Chicken & Waffles and Thai crispy fish with Thai chilli sauce too.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Decided to treat my friend for her birthday at a place which i believe both of us could enjoy. Heard of this place since 2022 i believe but never had a chance to come previously. When i step in, i could feel good vibes with a good choice of song list i could say, clean and cozy with nice decor. We've been greeted and attended by Azrul upon our arrival and  been ushered to out seat. While seating i found the atmosphere was very nice and calm/chill which very comforting. I find their menu is very interesting since they offered Thai and Western food (some even a fusion of both). After ordering our food, i approached their counter to ask about the cakes that they offered and been attended by Sheryln, since i wanted to surprise my friend and Sheryln guide the how to. Overall we were stuffed with good foods served by both Azrul and Sheryln which is quite friendly and professional. Its definitely a good choice on my side (self praised, lol).

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Admiralty MRT (NS10) is an elevated station on Singapore's North-South Line (NSL) in the heart of Woodlands, known for its seamless integration with Kampung Admiralty, an innovative senior-focused development featuring housing, a medical centre, and a hawker centre. It serves as a vital transport hub connecting residents via MRT and bus to the city and other parts of Singapore, offering amenities like shops and food options within its premises and nearby Causeway Point, reflecting the area's transition from a former naval base to a modern, family-friendly hub.
Key Features:
Line: North-South Line (NSL).
Operator: SMRT Corporation.
Structure: Elevated, with two tracks and two platforms.
Connectivity: Excellent links to Woodlands, Yishun, and the Central area; near expressways (SLE, BKE).
Accessibility: Wheelchair accessible with bicycle facilities.
Notable Integrations:
Kampung Admiralty: Directly linked, this unique vertical village combines public housing for seniors with a medical centre, childcare, and a hawker centre.
Causeway Point: A major shopping mall adjacent to the station, offering extensive retail and dining.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
One of the older stations in NSL. Simple, straightforward, elevator and escalator accesses, and connect directly to Admiralty Place. Not exactly a mall, but a consolidation of many facilities like wet market, supermarkets, eateries, coffee places, pharmacies, doctors, money changer, fast food.

However, while there are many atm machines here, there is no bank here. There used to be an UOB Bank but it has been replaced.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Admiralty mrt is now a very convenient place with lots of amenities and food. Just in front of the mrt station is the  Admiralty Place mall and Kampong Admiralty hawker centre. Within the vicinity, you can find 3 supermarkets - 24hr Fairprice, Prime and Giant and also a wet market.
You can also find a Cantine food court, KFC, Subway, A&W and a large 2 storey McDonald's here. Overall, this makes Admiralty a much more pleasant and convenient place for the residents living here.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Admiralty MRT (NS10) is an elevated station on Singapore's North-South Line (NSL) in the heart of Woodlands, known for its seamless integration with Kampung Admiralty, an innovative senior-focused development featuring housing, a medical centre, and a hawker centre. It serves as a vital transport hub connecting residents via MRT and bus to the city and other parts of Singapore, offering amenities like shops and food options within its premises and nearby Causeway Point, reflecting the area's transition from a former naval base to a modern, family-friendly hub.
Key Features:
Line: North-South Line (NSL).
Operator: SMRT Corporation.
Structure: Elevated, with two tracks and two platforms.
Connectivity: Excellent links to Woodlands, Yishun, and the Central area; near expressways (SLE, BKE).
Accessibility: Wheelchair accessible with bicycle facilities.
Notable Integrations:
Kampung Admiralty: Directly linked, this unique vertical village combines public housing for seniors with a medical centre, childcare, and a hawker centre.
Causeway Point: A major shopping mall adjacent to the station, offering extensive retail and dining.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
One of the older stations in NSL. Simple, straightforward, elevator and escalator accesses, and connect directly to Admiralty Place. Not exactly a mall, but a consolidation of many facilities like wet market, supermarkets, eateries, coffee places, pharmacies, doctors, money changer, fast food.

However, while there are many atm machines here, there is no bank here. There used to be an UOB Bank but it has been replaced.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
Jurong West Swimming Complex — Hidden Gem Alert

Best Sunday experience, hands down. Not crazy crowded, which is a rare win in Singapore. The lap pool is bliss—can actually swim freely without dodging elbows. Bonus: it’s shaded from the sun, so no need to fry while doing laps.

Even under CAT1 lightning alerts, you can still swim (huge plus). The kids’ pool is a comfortable 0.9m depth, perfect for little ones. And yes—warm showers in the children’s facilities. Game changer.

There’s a decent water park setup for kids and a surprisingly long water slide that’s way more fun than expected. Honestly, this place is the most underrated swimming complex in Singapore. Total hidden gem. Don’t sleep on it.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
888 Plaza was a genuine surprise for me. On the surface it looks like a quiet neighbourhood mall, but step inside and it quietly flexes. You have everything here. DIY shops, fast food, proper hawker fare, and those in-between stalls that you did not know you needed until you saw them.
It is the kind of place where you can buy household essentials, grab a quick kopi, settle a full meal, and still wander around without feeling rushed or overwhelmed. Nothing flashy, nothing pretentious, just practical variety done right.
What I liked most is how functional it is for daily life. Families, seniors, students, everyone seems to have a reason to be there. Food options are solid, prices are reasonable, and it feels very lived-in in the best possible way.
Not a destination mall, but absolutely a dependable one. Sometimes that is exactly what you want.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
A large park not far from the Republic Polytechnic and the upcoming new RTS terminus of Woodlands North. It's also the gateway to Woodlands Waterfront Jetty and its seafood restaurant. The premise encompasses the large playground with multiple slides, some as high as the ridge surrounding the playground itself, and the mangrove forest.

An interesting fact I found during my visit - the playground has the rare swing for wheelchair users, although this was inoperable in time being.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Came here 7 years ago when it was new, and the place was nice and new. Kids had a lot of fun there. 7 years later, the place is so fun down, and most of the slides are closed for repairs. There's also green moss on every surface. Facilities were no better, the toilet is not very clean and everything was just not well maintained. Really night and day. Hope the repairs bring it back to its former glory.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
A decent-sized park in the north, around Woodland neighborhood. What I like about this park is the feel. Going inside into it, it takes me out of the urban space where I can usually enjoy the nature without feeling that I am in the middle of the city.

There are multiple entrances into this park. For those who wants to take bus, there is a direct bus from Woodlands interchange where one can alight near Woodland waterfront, if I remember correctly. We used to come here quite often for macro shots, taking pictures of insects, hunching our backs over some shrubs.

For those who enjoy walking, this can be a decent walking/jogging route. Some parts are quite flat, while near the end where the mangrove is more hilly with some ups and downs and even the ramp type road on a spot (picture).

When I came here again (March 2026) it still feels nice, but some maintenance is needed. Missing and rusty signages, stagnant water, some trash (this has to be attributed to irresponsible visitors), but other than that, it still looks like what I remember, except for some ongoing maintenances and constructions around the park.

There was a sign of Bridge C closing (near the mangrove), but it was small and located in the middle of a shelter that I didn’t stop by because I didn’t need to take a rest. As a result, I missed the announcement and walked all the way to the Mangrove Forest from the wrong trail. I ended up in front of the hoarded up area and had to circle back through another trail until I found the mangrove and the other side of the hoarding.

I saw a bunch of monkeys, a bunch of otters, a big monitor lizards, and since my main purpose is to walk, I didn’t go for macro hunt. There is a big playground in the park, a water lily pond, mangrove forest, and nice natural trails. Overall, I like this park because it still feels natural and it’s not that crowded or popular so I can still enjoy a nice walks. During weekdays, there are older folks. The monkeys are respectful, leave them alone and they won’t bother you. Not sure if this are different groups or the ones we usually saw back then.

There are playgrounds, ampitheatre, mangrove inside here.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Beautiful nature and a great route for jogging! Wish the park could’ve been more preserved as you can tell that it’s been awhile since someone came to
do any cleaning. Citizen needs to clear their own trash as it can be seen everywhere which will definitely affect the wildlife there.

Would be great if we can install lightings for night walk/run as it can get pitch black once the sun set.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: This park offers a wonderful setting for a family outing. Children can delight in the available slides and swings. There is also ample space provided for enjoyable cycling and jogging. It is a truly pleasant environment for outdoor activities.

review_text:
The location is very hard to get to if you're from other parts of Singapore, but if you're from the area, you'd probably say it's convenient given how close it is to Causeway Point.

We came here only for our Moderna booster jab, and rarely make our way here. I must say, I'm very impressed by the Raffles Medical team that facilitated the vaccination exercise. Queues were fast, people were nice, and we were done in a super quick time.

We had the 730pm appointment, took 3 minutes to get our queue number, jab, and sit at the waiting area. Another 15 minutes of instituted waiting time, and off you go, you're done. It's literally like a solid factory line, which is a little stressful but efficient.

Thanks team! I'm rarely at Woodlands but you made my evening, and my rare road trip up North, a little better.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Dear Management Team,
I am writing to share a truly exceptional experience I recently had at your store.
While shopping for meat at GIANT Food, I was warmly welcomed by an outstanding employee whose name tag identified him as Mr. Youn Sang. From the moment I approached the counter, he made my entire experience feel special and valued.
What impressed me most was his attentiveness to everyone around him. No customer in line was left unattended. Mothers with children were acknowledged with patience and kindness, and every single person received a warm, personalized greeting. His professionalism, positive energy, and genuine care for customers truly stood out.
It is rare to encounter someone who takes such pride in delivering exceptional service. Mr. Youn Sang made my shopping experience not only pleasant but memorable, and I felt it was important that your management team be made aware of the remarkable job he is doing.
Please extend my sincere appreciation to him. Employees like Mr. Youn Sang are a tremendous asset to your organization.
Sincerely,
Jay.C

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Worst hypermarket experience. Only three checkout counters were open on a Saturday night, which naturally led to long queues. We waited about 40 minutes just to check out. If the store is short-staffed, it should reconsider operating 24 hours. Giant, please learn from Sheng Siong.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: This is one of the best mega warehouses in Singapore to shop on the East side as it carries many brands of food, household n electrical items . The layout is good where one can shop in comfort. There is a food court that has a nice range of food where one can indulge after the shopping. A great place to stock up essentials for home as the ample parking is convenient to trolley ones purchase to the car. One can find wonderful bargains as the store holds promotional product brands that are a steal. Happy shopping , happy family.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Today, January 12, 2026, at the Giant food court, I ordered *mee hoon* soto from the Nasi Padang stall, asking for an extra chicken add-on for $2.00. After I collected the food, it was not hot and contained only about one tablespoon of chicken. I asked the lady, who sounded Indonesian, about it. She was not happy when I asked her if the $2.00 chicken had been added. She pulled the bowl, and not wanting to create a scene, I just pulled it back and left. I'm very disappointed with her attitude; at the very least, she could have explained nicely. This will be the last time.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Final Giant Hypemarket at Tampines ✔️✔️✔️✔️✔️👍👍👍

Giant Hypermarket at Tampines is ideal for one-stop grocery needs if you live in this area. There is a wide range of electronics, kitchenware, groceries, a semi-wet market, fruits, and vegetables. The prices are reasonable. There are also food stalls serving plenty of local dishes. Halal and vegetarian options are available and typically served until around 9:30 PM. Additionally, there is ample parking space with free parking available. If you live in this area, it’s an excellent place to shop for your daily needs.
Chris
17 Sept 2025 11am

Visiting Woodlands MRT Station during the early days of the Thomson-East Coast Line’s operation was an exciting experience, offering a glimpse into Singapore’s ever-evolving public transport landscape. A must-visit for transport enthusiasts and a welcome improvement for commuters in the area.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
I really love woodlands MRT and how convenient it is for me to use the transport to travel but I am putting this review 1 star because you guys need to fix the aircon for the shops inside the MRT (north south line)!!! The workers are sweating like crazy. Please fix the aircon, it’s so hot.

It’s not just that air con that is down in one shop, it’s multiple shops. Miniso, Popeyes, Rotikek, POSB and etc

Please fix the aircon!! I can literally feel the heat of the shop whenever I walk pass them. Also the workers have to install multiple fans for themselves and customers. If ticket service and passenger service have aircon, surely the shops can have AC too right?😢 I have high hopes for SMET and believe in you guys so please do something about it

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Did not personally visit the gym but wanted to give my sincerest thanks to their wonderful staff Adelin and Fariz (apologies if I misspelled your names), who helped my father during a bad fall outside the gym premise. Both staff members took prompt action upon seeing his fall and immediately brought a first aid kit and oxygen mask, reassuring my father while we were waiting for the ambulance. Thank you again 🙏

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Big field with alot of activities that I can do there, the gym is usually packed and it is right beside a swimming pool so I can cool off after a gym session. However, the distance taken to walk there is very long as it is very far away from woodlands mrt so that's why it's not a 5 star.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Nov 2021 update: just renovated. Feel much larger than before. Only 1 toilet inside the gym, and 2 changing room though. Looks more modern.

review_text: Excellent place to do your laps. They have a nice locker room, lockers for hire at 40 cents a go, benches to lay down on or place your stuff. review_text: Excellent place to do your laps. They have a nice locker room, lockers for hire at 40 cents a go, benches to lay down on or place your stuff. The life guards are always watching out for you and I felt safe doing my weekly swim there. Do follow the ground rules though to go with the flow.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Did not personally visit the gym but wanted to give my sincerest thanks to their wonderful staff Adelin and Fariz (apologies if I misspelled your names), who helped my father during a bad fall outside the gym premise. Both staff members took prompt action upon seeing his fall and immediately brought a first aid kit and oxygen mask, reassuring my father while we were waiting for the ambulance. Thank you again 🙏

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Big field with alot of activities that I can do there, the gym is usually packed and it is right beside a swimming pool so I can cool off after a gym session. However, the distance taken to walk there is very long as it is very far away from woodlands mrt so that's why it's not a 5 star.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: I visited here for a morning walk. The park is quite large, so you'll definitely work up a sweat walking around. I highly recommend bringing a water bottle. It's full of nature, and spotting a wild chicken was a fun experience.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Relaxing spot, perfect for a nature retreat while in the city. A lot less crowded than you'd expect, considering it's central spot.

It's a collection of spots really, rather than a single destination, with lifestyle dining, museums, educational spice gardens, wedding photo destinations, or just a plain picnic spot. It's much less of a climb than you'd think, is great all around.

View from the top is a comforting one, nice to see the cityscape.

One interesting thing is how it's occupied mainly by maids, foreign workers, and tourists. As an aside, Chinese tourists and their themed outfits are kinda fun! Maybe we should look into that. Under-utilized by Singaporeans - thats your tax dollars at work you know, might as well use it.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: This is a quiet green park in Singapore. Looks like an old park with lot of greenery. It's clam, quiet and with lot of shade its ideal for a relaxing time. The famous Fort canning tree tunnel is also located here. Entrance is free. Popular place amongst the tourists. Good place to relax.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Fort Canning Park is a lush green destination right in the heart of the city, notable for its tranquil atmosphere and historical significance. It's perfect for strolling, relaxing, and taking photos, especially with its famous "tree tunnel" viewpoint.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Visited in the late morning before the weather got too hot , historical park with heritage buildings & a gallery of history of this place was event fully refreshing .

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Refurbished recently. Some nice deco outside but not much has changed.
The net playground is still there, which is a good thing and a unique attraction to this mall.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
🟠 City Square Mall
180 Kitchener Road
Singapore 208539

🟣 Location =  🟣 the mall is located at the junction of Serangoon Road and Kitchener Road.

🟣 It is directly connected to the Farrer Park MRT station via B1 level passage-way.
______________________________________________
⬛️  Historical Context: The mall actually sits on the former site of the New World Amusement Park, which was a famous landmark in the Jalan Besar area for decades.

⬛️  When I was a child in the early 1970s, my late Peranakan father used to bring me to New World to jalan jalan.
________________________________________________
🟠 The mall had undergone a SGD50 million massive revamp from September 2023 to early 2026.

🟠  The revamp added 26,000 sq ft of space, featuring a new 24,000 sq ft "Gastro Square" Level 4 dining area, new playgrounds, and classy luxurious interior upgrades.

🟨 The perimeters ( outside stretch of the mall)
*************************************************
🟨  There is a small verdant green park at the back of the mall, where a decomissioned MRT train cabin sits. ▪︎It is a hub for educational purposes.

🟨 There is also a small Merry-Go-Round & a Tea Cup Spinning Ride, reminiscent of the rides I used to enjoy in the 1970s.

🟩 For the interior of the mall itself
************************************
➡️ Below Info ➡️  is from the lift directory panel.

🟩L1 = City Green

🟩 B1 = Underpass to MRT, Taxi Stand, Pick-Up & Drop-Off, Green Square/ NTUC Fairprice/ Q&M Medical & Dental

🟩 B2=  ATM / Customer Service Counter/ Don Donki

🟩 B3=  Car Park + Food Square + Food Republic

🟩 B4= Car Park

⬛️ L9 To L6 = Schools & Offices  + Singapore Sustainability Academy (SSA)

⬛️ L5 = Discovery Square & Golden Village Cinema

⬛️ L4=  Gastro Square & CONNECT @ City Square Mall ( 🔴5 social service agencies)

⬛️ L 3 =  MST Golf Super Store

⬛️ L2 =  Decathlon & Uniqlo
________________________________________
🔴 Important info:
*******************
🔴 Level 4 features a CONNECT at City Square Mall, a dedicated community space comprising 5 ( five) social service agencies, affiliated with NCSS ( ▪︎National Council of Social Services), which itself is a statutory board under MSF.

🔴 MSF = Ministry of Social and Family Development.

🔴 The five agencies on Level 4 are
**************************************
● Counselling and Care Centre
● Kampong Kapor Community Services
● Singapore Children’s Society
● The Salvation Army

● Eagles Mediation & Counselling Centre ➡️ at B2 level

➖️ Dated 24 March 2026/ Tuesday.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
If you are around Little India area, City Square Mall is actually a pretty nice place to chill and makan.
It's just a short walk from Mustafa Centre and the mall has a good mix of food spots, from quick bites to proper restaurants and food court options. Halal food choices are quite easy to find too.

Shopping wise, it's the kind of mall that feels practical and comfortable. Got everyday stores like Uniqlo and Decathlon, so you can actually shop without everything feeling overly expensive.

Bonus point for tea lovers… theres Beutea too🧋

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
It’s a nice mall and came here because to buy a drink from the nice supermarket. There is a Rest n Go area where there are many massage chairs. You have to scan the QR code to make the payment first and select which package. It’s worth as paying for $15 and 70mins of relaxation! Even paying $10 and 45mins of relaxation is good too. There were many willing to pay and relax on the chairs here and that’s good.

Latest update! A wonderful discovery and if you’re looking for a barber nearby this mall, there’s one! Jerudili Barber and it’s operated by a passionate with professionalism guy. He takes care of your hair well and really comfortable. He cuts hair great too! Please do support since it’s quite new. It’s at 681 Race Course Road and exit F, once alighted from Farrer Park MRT station. I have attached the barber shop photo too which includes the address.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
It’s a very big mall with many renowned international brands like Levi’s, Breadtalk, and toy stores for the little ones like ToysRUs etc. Also sometimes there is a flea market inside the mall with some cool toys and books for a great price. Lots of restaurants as well in easy access.

Outside there is a musical park where you can play some soothing music or relax on the hammocks and there is also a eco bus, with environmental activities and it also spreads awareness about the environment. In all, it’s a great mall!

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
We visited IMM Building, Singapore’s largest outlet mall, and had a good shopping experience. This place really lives up to what many reviewers say — it has all varieties from high-end brands to low-end shops, making it easier to find what you need under one roof. You can discover everything from fashion and sportswear to electronics, home goods, and plenty of dining options.

Many visitors also love the discounts on big brands (up to around 80% at outlet stores), and the mall is spacious and easy to navigate. There are lots of food and beverage outlets to take a break while shopping.

Crowds can get heavier on weekends, and some items in outlet stores may not be the latest season stock, but overall it’s a great place for bargain hunting and casual shopping.

Overall experience was pleasant — 4 stars ⭐⭐⭐⭐.

Hum log IMM Building gaye aur overall shopping experience kaafi achhi rahi. Jaise online reviews mein bhi log likhte hain, yahan high-end se leke low-end tak sab varieties mil jaati hain, jis se shopping easy ho jati hai — fashion, sportswear, electronics, home goods aur bahut saare food options sab ek hi jagah par.

Bohot saare visitors ko big brands par ache discounts bhi milte hain aur mall ka layout kaafi spacious hai jisme easily explore kar sakte ho.

Weekend par thoda crowd zyada ho sakta hai aur outlet stores mein sab items latest season ke nahi milte, lekin overall yeh mall bargain hunting aur casual shopping ke liye ek achhi jagah hai.

Overall experience achha raha — 4 stars ⭐⭐⭐⭐.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Singapore's only outlet mall with all major brands under one roof. You will get good deals from top brands here. Don't forget to plan to visit here when you travel to Singapore. Must visit mall.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
IMM Mall is a great spot for shopping, especially if you’re looking for outlet deals. It has a wide range of stores, including brands like Coach, Michael Kors, Kate Spade, Charles & Keith, Pedro, Cotton On, and plenty of footwear outlets.
Getting there is easy—take the MRT to Jurong East MRT Station and follow the signs. It’s about a 10 minute walk, and you’ll pass JEM on the way.
There are also lots of dining options, making it a good place to spend a few hours. If you’re shopping, if you spend over SGD 100 in a store, you can request a GST refund receipt—just show your passport at the time of purchase. You can then claim the refund at the airport during departure.
Overall, a solid choice if you’re planning a shopping trip in Singapore.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
For my family and me living in the west, IMM is one of our our go-to spots for shopping, leisure, and meals because it is just so convenient.
Also, being directly connected to Jurong East MRT and the Bus Interchange makes it exceptionally accessible.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Have visited IMM since I was a kid. That was like decades ago. Since then, new wings were constructed, parking has expanded, new links to the Jurong shopping cluster has been built and stores have come and go. Really like how there everything here, from daily necessities to outlet stores. Weekend parking is a nightmare though and the gantry should be upgraded to reduce the jam at the carpark entrance. Also, glad that level 3 is pet friendly thanks to the new BYD cafe.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
One of the old malls that had been revamped.

We were here years ago and after coming back bout 15years later, the place looks really different!

Lets dive in!

Location:
Integrated with the bus interchange and mrt, this mall is considered a hub for residents living in woodlands. There are plenty of entrances and is easily accessible everywhere.

Amenities:
Other than a furniture shop, you can find almost everything here. We were in awe and the food selections that we can find here. Not forgetting the gadget shops that have competitive prices. They also have a prayer room, which we were glad to use.

Parking:
Despite the many accesible options around, this mall has lots of parking lots available., subjected to non-peak hours yeah😅. Alternatively, there are also other nearby areas to park actually.

All in all, if you are up for an exercise to walk, this is one place to head down to. After walking on all floors, treat yourself to shopping and a good meal too!

Highly recommended from us!

Cheers!

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Explored Causeway Point in a day. Anchor tenants like Metro Store, Courts for electrical and furniture, Uniqlo Japanese fashion wear. Lots of F&B selections from Kopitiam Bagus halal food court, Food Republic food court, Chinese, Japanese, Korean & western cuisines including lots of fried chicken - Texas, KFC, Jollibee, 4Fingers , Chagee, and lots of BbTea shops. Bakeries & Patisserie like Swee Heng, Annabelle, rrooll. You can spend a whole day exploring beautiful home decor at Madera and kitchen needs. To pamper yourselves with a pair of new glasses or shades from the few optical stores as well as beauty salons for hair and nails. Nostalgic snacks store, automated machines for the SGFR viral snack, Popmart machines can be found too.  From Causeway Point basement it will lead you to Woods Square and Woodlands Civic Center. Highly recommended to visit.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Wow many eateries at the basement offering good food here. Also first floor have a lot of nice shops selling food for takeaway all in one shopping mall.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Causeway Point is one of the older heartland malls in Singapore. Located just outside Woodlands MRT station, which is now an interchange to TEL line, and a bus interchange, it is in a very strategic location which explains why it’s never not busy. Causeway Point is also connected to Woodlands Civic Center and Woods Square. Beside it is also a location for occasional temporary Pasar Malam.

So, if you don’t like people, this place can be hellish. On the other side, it’s also a very convenient place to get everything you need from grocery, stationery, household items, electronic, pet stuff, food, everything you need.

The mall has everything, from Cinema to Yakun, Popular bookstore to FairPrice supermarket. There are many food varieties to choose from including two food courts. There are Metro department store and Courts. There are pharmacies like Watson, Guardian, and Unity.

Pros: You can find almost everything you need.

Cons: People! So many people!

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Love ❤️ Causeway Point.

It’s near my house 🏠 and it has a wide variety of stores under one roof. Satisfy all my needs.

My daughter and I often went there to eat.

Lots of F&B selections from Kopitiam Bagus halal food court, Food Republic food court, Chinese, Japanese, Korean & western cuisines including lots of fried chicken - Texas, KFC, Jollibee, 4Fingers , Chagee, and lots of BbTea shops. Bakeries & Patisserie like Swee Heng, Annabelle, rrooll. You can spend a whole day exploring beautiful home decor at Madera and kitchen needs. To pamper yourselves with a pair of new glasses or shades from the few optical stores as well as beauty salons for hair and nails. Nostalgic snacks store, automated machines for the SGFR viral snack, Popmart machines can be found too.  From Causeway Point basement it will lead you to Woods Square and Woodlands Civic Center. Highly recommended to visit.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Wow many eateries at the basement offering good food here. Also first floor have a lot of nice shops selling food for takeaway all in one shopping mall.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Causeway Point is one of the older heartland malls in Singapore. Located just outside Woodlands MRT station, which is now an interchange to TEL line, and a bus interchange, it is in a very strategic location which explains why it’s never not busy. Causeway Point is also connected to Woodlands Civic Center and Woods Square. Beside it is also a location for occasional temporary Pasar Malam.

So, if you don’t like people, this place can be hellish. On the other side, it’s also a very convenient place to get everything you need from grocery, stationery, household items, electronic, pet stuff, food, everything you need.

The mall has everything, from Cinema to Yakun, Popular bookstore to FairPrice supermarket. There are many food varieties to choose from including two food courts. There are Metro department store and Courts. There are pharmacies like Watson, Guardian, and Unity.

Pros: You can find almost everything you need.

Cons: People! So many people!

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Love ❤️ Causeway Point.

It’s near my house 🏠 and it has a wide variety of stores under one roof. Satisfy all my needs.

My daughter and I often went there to eat.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
One of the old malls that had been revamped.

We were here years ago and after coming back bout 15years later, the place looks really different!

Lets dive in!

Location:
Integrated with the bus interchange and mrt, this mall is considered a hub for residents living in woodlands. There are plenty of entrances and is easily accessible everywhere.

Amenities:
Other than a furniture shop, you can find almost everything here. We were in awe and the food selections that we can find here. Not forgetting the gadget shops that have competitive prices. They also have a prayer room, which we were glad to use.

Parking:
Despite the many accesible options around, this mall has lots of parking lots available., subjected to non-peak hours yeah😅. Alternatively, there are also other nearby areas to park actually.

All in all, if you are up for an exercise to walk, this is one place to head down to. After walking on all floors, treat yourself to shopping and a good meal too!

Highly recommended from us!

Cheers!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: PlayFACTO School @ Woods Square | Student Care Woodlands
review_text:
I visited Playfacto Woods Square last year and met Ms Celine and Ms Wind. I love how cosy the centre feels and how amicable the ladies were.

I placed my boy in for adhoc care. On his first day, the teacher fetches him from the lobby and brought him to the centre safely. During the day, they also updated me how my boy was coping.

When I picked my boy, he shared that he like the teacher Ms Lee Lee and the student care very much! And he finds the food delicious too!
(I guess better than mummy cook 🤣)

As a parent, I feel assured that my child is in good hands and that he receives kind attention and care from the teachers. Thank you to all the staff for your heart ❤️ work in ensuring the children have the most comforting and nurturing environment after school. 👍👍👍

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: PlayFACTO School @ Woods Square | Student Care Woodlands
review_text:
I recently had the pleasure of visiting this beautiful center managed by a friendly and cheerful family. From the moment I arrived, I was greeted with warm smiles and a genuine sense of hospitality.

The center itself was lovely with lots of natural light from the ceiling to floor windows, and the interior design was tastefully done. It created a warm and inviting atmosphere that made me feel right at home. The attention to detail was evident in every corner, from the carefully selected decor to the comfortable seating areas.

Overall I must say, it was a delightful experience

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: PlayFACTO School @ Woods Square | Student Care Woodlands
review_text: Had a very pleasant experience at the PlayFacto Open House @ Woodsquare this morning. Ms Celine and her team of teachers have been very welcoming and shared comprehensively on the curriculum at the centre. Such a warm and cosy envrionment that we decided to sign up immediately. :)

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: PlayFACTO School @ Woods Square | Student Care Woodlands
review_text: Excellent service right from the pre-registration phase, answering all inquiries promptly and giving sound advice throughout. Looking forward to my children joining them tomorrow onwards. Been to the place in person and it's very child friendly and cozy.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: PlayFACTO School @ Woods Square | Student Care Woodlands
review_text: You should send your primary school kids here! This place is all positive vibes the real stars are the teachers and the centre director Ms Celine. They’re super friendly and warm, provide excellent updates on the progress of the little ones, and genuinely care about the kids. The place feels like a modern playground‑slash‑learning studio, with big sunny rooms and a really cosy vibe that the kids love so much. You have to see it for yourself! The teacher‑to‑kid ratio is also perfect and the little ones get all the help they need and finish homework before pick up. You’re paying for peace of mind at this centre, knowing your kid’s happy, learning, and super well cared for!

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Woodlands Waterfront Park is a relaxing and scenic park located in the northern part of Singapore. It faces the Straits of Johor, so visitors can actually see Malaysia across the water. The park covers around 11 hectares and has a 1.5-kilometre waterfront promenade, which makes it a great place for walking, jogging, or cycling while enjoying the sea breeze.
One of the most interesting features of the park is the 400-metre Woodlands Waterfront Jetty, which is the northernmost point of mainland Singapore. Walking along the jetty is a really nice experience because you get a wide view of the sea and the coastline. Many people go there to fish, take photos, or just relax while looking at the view. In the evening, the sunset from the jetty is especially beautiful and creates a calm atmosphere.
In my opinion, the park is a great place to visit if you want to take a break from the busy city. The long paths are perfect for a peaceful walk, and the cool sea breeze makes the environment very comfortable. There are also open spaces where families can have picnics, as well as a playground where children can play and climb.
Another thing I like about the park is that it is usually less crowded compared to many other parks in Singapore. Because of this, it feels quieter and more relaxing. Overall, Woodlands Waterfront Park is a wonderful place to enjoy nature, spend time with family and friends, and appreciate the beautiful waterfront scenery.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
This seaside park has an interesting history. The jetty, which is the centerpiece of the park, was a mooring location for the Malayan/Malaysian Navy when Singapore was still within the federation. It's surprising that the plot of land was still in the hand of Malaysian government till it's handed over in 1980s (almost 20 years upon the secession). The bollards at the jetty point out to the place's not-so-distant past.

Since the construction of the RTS train, it has been pretty popular place for the locals to take selfie, with the backdrop of the viaduct. Funnily enough, in spite of advisory against fishing at the jetty (posted all over), seems this is just a live and let live policy lol.

Woodlands Waterfront Park is a relaxing and scenic park located in the northern part of Singapore. It faces the Straits of Johor, so visitors can actually see Malaysia across the water. The park covers around 11 hectares and has a 1.5-kilometre waterfront promenade, which makes it a great place for walking, jogging, or cycling while enjoying the sea breeze.
One of the most interesting features of the park is the 400-metre Woodlands Waterfront Jetty, which is the northernmost point of mainland Singapore. Walking along the jetty is a really nice experience because you get a wide view of the sea and the coastline. Many people go there to fish, take photos, or just relax while looking at the view. In the evening, the sunset from the jetty is especially beautiful and creates a calm atmosphere.
In my opinion, the park is a great place to visit if you want to take a break from the busy city. The long paths are perfect for a peaceful walk, and the cool sea breeze makes the environment very comfortable. There are also open spaces where families can have picnics, as well as a playground where children can play and climb.
Another thing I like about the park is that it is usually less crowded compared to many other parks in Singapore. Because of this, it feels quieter and more relaxing. Overall, Woodlands Waterfront Park is a wonderful place to enjoy nature, spend time with family and friends, and appreciate the beautiful waterfront scenery.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
This seaside park has an interesting history. The jetty, which is the centerpiece of the park, was a mooring location for the Malayan/Malaysian Navy when Singapore was still within the federation. It's surprising that the plot of land was still in the hand of Malaysian government till it's handed over in 1980s (almost 20 years upon the secession). The bollards at the jetty point out to the place's not-so-distant past.

Since the construction of the RTS train, it has been pretty popular place for the locals to take selfie, with the backdrop of the viaduct. Funnily enough, in spite of advisory against fishing at the jetty (posted all over), seems this is just a live and let live policy lol.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: It's a beautiful place we can see Singapore and Malaysia at that same time. When I went it's full rainy and I took photoshoot there. We want to spend peace area I will suggest The Woodlands Waterfront. Beautiful silent area. We will hear only Air sound, Water sound.

review_text:
Here's a review you can use:

---

Ryan chou
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

Woodlands Waterfront Park is one of my favorite spots to unwind. The park offers a beautiful, relaxing view of the Johor Strait, especially during sunset. The waterfront promenade is perfect for a peaceful stroll or a bike ride, and there’s plenty of open space for picnics with family and friends.

I also love the iconic jetty—it’s a great place to take in the sea breeze and watch the boats go by. The park is well-maintained, with clean paths and plenty of benches to sit and enjoy the scenery. Highly recommended for anyone looking to escape the city hustle without going too far!

review_text: It was my first time in th Woodlands MRT station straight from the Woodlands North Singapore immigration. The station is very accessible by the bus coming from the Woodlands North and easy to get to. I also love that there's Jollibee there 😍.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Woodlands MRT Station 🚉 always have a lot of passengers but it is always orderly, clean and neat.

Love ❤️ that it has many eating establishments so provide a lot of convenience to passengers who just got out of the station and can easily grab dinner on their way home 🏠.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Woodlands MRT has become a bigger hub now that it connects to TEL Mrt Line that goes into the ciry and hopefully not too far in the future, to the Changi airport.

It's also a big bus interchange, with the aircon indoor one, and the old bus interchange now serves as ‘temporary’ bus interchange although it has been temporary for years.

The MRT is also connected to a heartland mall Causeway Point, which connects to Woodlands Civic center and Wood Square. Daily necessities, supermarket, F&B, fashion, electronic, book store, library, coffee, cinema. It practically has almost everything necessary.

There are several F&B options in the MRT station itself, but to be honest, most of them are not good options, but they are still okay for quick bite.

During certain timing of the year a pasar malam would be opened on the field next to the Causeway Point.

Since it's a hub, except this place to be very busy, full of people and traffic. The flow can be confusing, but there are many directional signs available. The food option for early morning crowd is very limited though, even on the weekend, but that is normal everywhere.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Clean, spacious and trains run regularly. Only issue is a common one across stations across the world: clear signage. Sometimes too small, contradictory or unclear. It's a little confusing for any new visitors.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Really like how spacious the interior is, and how the red and brown line links underground to its multiple exit points.

Ventilation is good and facilities are new. Tel line takes an average of 5~7 mins to gome for every interval. But between stops is really fast. I take 30 mins to reach Orchard via TEL line, whereas red takes me 45 mins or so.

Down side is that TEL line closes quite early.

Interesting sharing on how numeracy was taught in class. Thank you teachers for the planning. A lot of effort is put into the class preparation.

The mrw workshop was engaging and informative. It provided deep insight to what the children are exposed to in the school. Thank you Ms Iqah and management for organising the session.

The events was engaging and exciting. The kids were very involved in the preparation, and managed to practice their fine motor skills and reinforced what they learnt in their lessons during the preparation phase.

The activities are planned based on the child's age and abilities. My kids love them and I had fun playing with them.

It was fun to engage in the world sleep day activities with my child. Thank you teachers for organising it.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Visited Chong Pang Hawker Centre recently and tried a few stalls… overall a really satisfying meal.

Started with Chuan Kee Boneless Braised Duck Rice. The duck was very tender and flavourful, with a nice herbal braised sauce that wasn’t too overpowering. I also liked that it was boneless, which made it easy to eat.

Then I had the char kway teow from Pin Xiang. Good wok hei, noodles were well-cooked and not overly greasy. Portion was decent and the flavours were balanced.

To finish, I got an ice blended watermelon juice from Fruit Palace. Very refreshing, especially in the heat… not too sweet and quite natural tasting.

Overall, a solid hawker experience. Would come back to try other stalls as well 👍

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: A wet market and hawker centre located in a local area under an HDB. People are very nice and welcoming. My go to every week for fresh fruit and vegetables. Hawker foods are amazing, from Chinese to Indian and every in between. Michelin Cendol here too and it is a must try!!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Good old hawker with a mox of variety of food choices. The famous Chew Kuah, Duck Rice, Fish soup and Porridge, delicious Nyonya Chendol are just a few to mention. Highly recommended.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Food lover can consider this places. But prepared to sweat if the weather turn hot! Today had tried this prawns noodle stall. First taste of the soup bring me back to my childhood life. Soup very authentic,  ingredients very fresh. And for the pork slice,  cooked to perfection tender.. I will come back for more . Alot of nice food too. Wish my stomach can eat from every stall. Hehehe. Only ＄4 for small bowl. But is more than enough for me.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Visited Chong Pang Hawker Centre recently and tried a few stalls… overall a really satisfying meal.

Started with Chuan Kee Boneless Braised Duck Rice. The duck was very tender and flavourful, with a nice herbal braised sauce that wasn’t too overpowering. I also liked that it was boneless, which made it easy to eat.

Then I had the char kway teow from Pin Xiang. Good wok hei, noodles were well-cooked and not overly greasy. Portion was decent and the flavours were balanced.

To finish, I got an ice blended watermelon juice from Fruit Palace. Very refreshing, especially in the heat… not too sweet and quite natural tasting.

Overall, a solid hawker experience. Would come back to try other stalls as well 👍

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: A wet market and hawker centre located in a local area under an HDB. People are very nice and welcoming. My go to every week for fresh fruit and vegetables. Hawker foods are amazing, from Chinese to Indian and every in between. Michelin Cendol here too and it is a must try!!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Good old hawker with a mox of variety of food choices. The famous Chew Kuah, Duck Rice, Fish soup and Porridge, delicious Nyonya Chendol are just a few to mention. Highly recommended.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Food lover can consider this places. But prepared to sweat if the weather turn hot! Today had tried this prawns noodle stall. First taste of the soup bring me back to my childhood life. Soup very authentic,  ingredients very fresh. And for the pork slice,  cooked to perfection tender.. I will come back for more . Alot of nice food too. Wish my stomach can eat from every stall. Hehehe. Only ＄4 for small bowl. But is more than enough for me.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Located very near where my Queen stays, we walked here for our morning walk as we decided to start a new healthier routine and hoping it will turn into a habit and lifestyle. All starting on 21st September 2025 10am :) it’s a soothing sunny morning, we see Malay Pakcik practicing Silat, very similar to Qi Gong, making use of sudden burst energy, spotted families playing swings, boys at the playground, beloved maids having picnics, saw pavilions, found a Gym Pod and many more. We tried out their fitness corners, and played at their interactive playground spots. Spotted an observatory here too, but didn’t climb up cos we wanted to save some leg power for the walk to breakfast. Overall, a very funny park, hope to visit more!

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
A Walk That Changed My View: Marsiling Park

I used to think of parks in Singapore as predictable—neatly landscaped, well-paved, and mostly meant for joggers or families with children. That was until I took a quiet walk one weekend morning through Marsiling Park.

Tucked away in the north, Marsiling Park doesn’t have the same fame as East Coast or Botanic Gardens, but perhaps that’s what makes it so special. As I wandered along its shaded paths, past the tranquil pond and over the wooden boardwalks, something shifted in me. The calm rustle of leaves, the subtle ripples in the water, the calls of kingfishers and mynahs—it all felt surprisingly immersive. The park wasn’t just a recreational space; it was a gentle, breathing reminder that nature still has its place in our urban rhythm.

What struck me most was how seamlessly Marsiling Park blended both serenity and surprise. Every turn offered a different mood: a sculpture quietly nestled among trees, a small bridge leading to reflections of sky and trees mirrored in the pond. It invited me not to rush, but to slow down and observe.

That walk transformed my perception of Singapore’s green spaces. It reminded me that our parks aren’t just backdrops for activity—they are living sanctuaries, places to reflect, recharge, and reconnect. Since then, I’ve made it a personal weekend ritual to explore different parks and trails across the island. It’s not about the steps or the distance anymore—it’s about feeling grounded, soaking in the textures of nature, and remembering that peace can often be found just a short walk away.

Walk at the park and enjoyed the moments of feeling grounded over the weekends before you kickstart another busy work week ahead! 🫶🏻💪

review_text: Don’t let the entrance deceive you. From the outside it may look a small and simple library. The lib actually do have 4 stories, and each story has a vast collection of books. Unlike other libraries, Woodland library may have the largest space all with comfy sofa or study desk. It also have a cafe if you need a quick coffee fix. Kids area upstairs.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Visited with my wife today and we were both thoroughly impressed. By far the biggest and nicest mall in JB that I’ve seen! The atmosphere is modern and upscale, making it feel more like a premium destination than just a shopping center.

There is a huge variety of international and luxury brands (SOGO, Uniqlo, MUJI) and the dining options are endless—from casual bites to high-quality restaurants. The layout is spacious and clean, which makes walking around very comfortable even when it's busy. If you only have time for one mall in JB, this is definitely the one to visit. Highly recommended!

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
​If you are looking for the ultimate shopping and dining destination in JB, The Mall, Mid Valley Southkey is hands-down the place to be. It feels like a slice of Kuala Lumpur’s retail luxury brought right to the South.

​Why It’s a Must-Visit:
-​Retail Therapy Galore: The mall is massive and incredibly well-curated. You’ll find everything from the high-end SOGO Department Store to flagship-sized favorites like Uniqlo, ZARA, and Cotton On. Whether you’re hunting for luxury goods or street style, you’re covered.

-​The Food Scene is Elite: This is a literal paradise for foodies. You can grab a quick fix at FUIYOH! It’s Uncle Roger for that signature fried rice, indulge in a brunch at The Founders Cafe, or brave the legendary queues for the famous egg tarts and Nasi Lemak at Oriental Kopi.

-​Modern & Spacious: Unlike some older malls, the layout here is bright, wide, and very easy to navigate. The air conditioning is a dream on a hot day, and the overall vibe is chic and upscale.

-​Great for Families: With the GSC Cinema, Kiddytopia, and an indoor theme park, there’s plenty to keep the kids entertained while you shop. Plus, the Village Grocer is perfect for picking up high-quality international snacks before heading home.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Mid Valley Southkey is one of the best shopping destinations in Johor Bahru. The mall is huge, modern, and very clean, with a great mix of international and local brands. There’s something for everyone — from fashion and electronics to cafés, restaurants, and entertainment options. The layout is spacious and easy to navigate, and parking is well-organized. I also liked that there are plenty of food choices, from casual bites to premium dining. A perfect spot to spend the day shopping, dining, or just relaxing.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
This mall is huge, luxurious, and futuristic. The design feels very open and spacious, with lots of greenery that makes the whole place feel fresh and relaxing 🌿

I spent about two hours there shopping and having a meal, and it was a really pleasant experience. The mall feels very clean and well-maintained, and it seems like they host events throughout the year.

You can probably find almost anything you want here, the place is so big that you could easily get lost walking around 🛍️

One thing to be careful about is rush hour traffic. We were stuck in traffic right in front of the mall for almost an hour around 6 PM, so it’s better to plan your visit earlier or later to avoid that 🚗

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: I love this place! This is my go-to shopping mall whenever I need to restock my makeup, toiletries, necessities and much more. SOGO is especially great for those looking to buy frangrances, shoes and etc. They have everything you need here!
